seoTitle: Commerce after 10th — courses, exams & careers | SkillKite
seoDescription: After 10th Commerce — streams, CA foundation path, B.Com track & more

guide.heading: Your options after 10th — SkillKite
guide.greeting: Hi there! You told us you are interested in commerce and want to both study and earn. The most relevant options for you are listed first — read through all of them before deciding.

sections.title: Study options
sections.intro: Most relevant option is listed first — but read all options before deciding.

options.name: 12th Commerce
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream covering Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, and optionally Mathematics or Informatics Practices. The most direct path for commerce-interested students.
options.whoFor: Students who enjoy business, money management, markets, and entrepreneurship.
options.leadsTo: B.Com, BBA, CA Foundation, CS Foundation, banking jobs, finance careers, and many government exams.
options.keyExams: CA Foundation (after or during 12th), CUET for central university admissions, state CET exams for BBA/B.Com.
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3 years for graduation.

options.name: Polytechnic Diploma (3 years, direct after 10th)
options.whatIsIt: A government-recognized technical diploma in fields like Computer Science, Civil, Mechanical, or IT. You can join directly after 10th without waiting for 12th.
options.whoFor: Students who want a practical skill-based qualification quickly and are open to technical fields alongside commerce.
options.leadsTo: Junior engineer roles, government jobs, or direct entry into 2nd year of B.Tech (lateral entry). Also good for self-employment.
options.keyExams: State-level polytechnic entrance exams (e.g., UP JEECUP, Bihar DCECE, Delhi CET). Admission criteria and competition vary by state.

options.name: 12th Science with Maths (PCM)
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. Rigorous and opens many engineering and technology doors.
options.whoFor: Students who are strong in Maths and want to keep engineering or technology options open. Less directly relevant if your core interest is commerce.
options.leadsTo: B.Tech/BE, NDA, architecture, data science, and various science-based careers.
options.keyExams: JEE Main, JEE Advanced, state CETs, NDA.
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 4 years for B.Tech.

options.name: 12th Science with Biology (PCB)
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. Focused on medical and life sciences.
options.whoFor: Students who want to become doctors, nurses, or work in healthcare. Not directly relevant to a commerce interest.
options.leadsTo: MBBS, BDS, BAMS, nursing, paramedical courses, and biotechnology.
options.keyExams: NEET-UG (very competitive — around 20 lakh students appear each year for limited seats).
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 4.5 to 5.5 years for MBBS.

options.name: 12th Science with Maths + Biology (PCMB)
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream that combines all four subjects — Physics, Chemistry, Maths, and Biology. Keeps both engineering and medical options open but is a heavy workload.
options.whoFor: Students who are genuinely undecided between medical and engineering and are confident they can handle extra subjects. Not the priority choice for a commerce-focused student.
options.leadsTo: MBBS, B.Tech, BSc, and more — but requires preparing for both JEE and NEET simultaneously.
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th — be aware the workload is significantly higher than other streams.

options.name: 12th Arts / Humanities
options.whatIsIt: A 2-year stream covering subjects like History, Political Science, Geography, Psychology, Sociology, and optionally Economics.
options.whoFor: Students drawn to social sciences, law, civil services, journalism, or teaching. Less aligned with a commerce interest but still a valid choice.
options.leadsTo: BA, LLB, civil services (IAS/IPS), journalism, education, social work, and creative fields.
options.keyExams: CUET, CLAT (for law), state university entrance exams.
options.timeCommitment: 2 years for 12th, then 3 years for graduation.

options.name: Paramedical Diploma after 10th (DMLT, ANM, X-Ray Tech, etc.)
options.whatIsIt: Short diploma programs in healthcare support roles like lab technician, nursing assistant, or X-ray technician. Available directly after 10th.
options.whoFor: Students who want to enter the healthcare sector quickly. This is not closely related to a commerce interest, but it is a legitimate option worth knowing about.
options.leadsTo: Jobs in hospitals, diagnostic labs, clinics, or government health departments.
options.keyExams: Mostly merit-based admissions; some states have entrance tests. Check your state's paramedical council.
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 2 years depending on the program.

sections.title: Job and earning options
sections.intro: Since you want to earn alongside studying, these are realistic skills and income paths you can start building right now — even before or during 12th. None of these require a college degree to begin.

options.name: Tally and Basic Accounting
options.whatIsIt: Learning Tally ERP and basic bookkeeping software used by almost every small business in India. Directly aligned with commerce.
options.whoFor: Commerce students who want to earn part-time or find quick jobs after 12th in local shops, businesses, or CA offices.
options.leadsTo: Part-time data entry jobs, junior accounts assistant roles, and strong support for CA articleship later.
options.keyExams: No formal exam required to start. Tally certification courses are available online and offline.
options.timeCommitment: 1 to 3 months to learn basics. You can start earning while studying 12th.

options.name: Meesho / Reselling and E-commerce
options.whatIsIt: Reselling products through platforms like Meesho, GlowRoad, or building a small Instagram shop. A practical introduction to real business and commerce.
options.whoFor: Students with a commerce mindset who want to understand buying, selling, margins, and customer handling firsthand.
options.leadsTo: Pocket income, real business experience, and a strong foundation for future entrepreneurship or an MBA.
options.timeCommitment: A few hours per week. Can run alongside school.

options.name: Content Creation (YouTube, Instagram Reels)
options.whatIsIt: Creating educational, entertaining, or business-related videos or posts on social media platforms to build an audience and earn through ads or brand deals.
options.whoFor: Students who are confident on camera or enjoy writing, and are willing to be consistent for several months before seeing income.
options.leadsTo: Ad revenue, brand collaborations, and digital marketing skills that are highly valued in business roles.
options.timeCommitment: Ongoing. Expect 6 to 12 months of consistent effort before meaningful income.

options.name: Graphic Design (Canva / Figma)
options.whatIsIt: Learning to create posters, social media graphics, and business marketing materials using tools like Canva (beginner-friendly) and Figma (intermediate).
options.whoFor: Students with a creative side who want to offer design services to local businesses, shops, or online clients.
options.leadsTo: Freelance income, social media management jobs, and a valuable skill for future marketing or business roles.
options.keyExams: None. Free and paid courses available on YouTube and platforms like Coursera.
options.timeCommitment: 2 to 4 weeks to learn Canva basics. Freelance work can begin shortly after.

options.name: Data Entry and Typing
options.whatIsIt: Providing data entry, form-filling, or document typing services to businesses, hospitals, or online clients. A simple entry-level earning option.
options.whoFor: Students who have decent typing speed and want stable, low-skill income while they focus on studies.
options.leadsTo: Part-time income. Can also build typing speed and computer familiarity useful for bank and government exams.
options.keyExams: None for freelance work. Government data entry roles require typing tests.
options.timeCommitment: A few hours per day. Easily combined with 12th studies.

options.name: Photography and Videography
options.whatIsIt: Offering photography or video services for local events like birthdays, weddings, and school functions using a smartphone or basic camera.
options.whoFor: Students with a good eye for visuals who want to earn from weekends and local events without major investment.
options.leadsTo: Freelance income, potential for a full business, and skills relevant to marketing and media careers.
options.timeCommitment: Learn basics in 1 to 2 months. Weekend work alongside school.

options.name: Mobile Phone Repair
options.whatIsIt: Learning to diagnose and fix common smartphone problems like screen replacement, battery issues, and software troubleshooting.
options.whoFor: Students who are hands-on and technically curious, and want a skill that can become a full business.
options.leadsTo: A repair shop, a mobile accessories business, or service center employment. High demand in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
options.keyExams: None. Short courses available at ITI centers and local training institutes.

options.name: Tailoring and Stitching
options.whatIsIt: Learning to stitch and alter garments professionally, which can lead to a home-based business or boutique work.
options.whoFor: Students who have an interest in fashion or want a skill that can run as a side income from home.
options.leadsTo: Home-based tailoring income, boutique work, or fashion design studies later.
options.keyExams: None. Courses available at local tailoring institutes and women's polytechnics.
options.timeCommitment: 3 to 6 months for a basic course.

guide.closingMessage: Save this guide and discuss it with your parents or teachers. When you finish 12th, come back to SkillKite — we'll build you a detailed career roadmap. Share it with a friend who needs it too. 🪁
